mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in declining rate periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer demand, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in >65% of triggered instances despite the rising unemployment signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized strength in consumer and housing demand, which amplifies revenue growth across cyclical sectors and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative forward returns in >55% of cases, or interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production reflects immediate supply-side bottlenecks and falling factory output, which triggers short-term risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of contraction periods during tightening cycles, or signal loses predictive power when inflation exceeds 3%.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation erodes real corporate margins and forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, which increases discount rates and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive forward returns occur in >50% of high-inflation trigger months, or inflation signal shows no rank correlation with returns.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls combined with a stable or declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market expansion, which supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns in >50% of cases, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product reflects stronger aggregate economic activity, which directly boosts corporate revenues and earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year rolling window or positive returns occur in <45% of high-GDP periods.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production during a post-recession recovery phase signals improving supply chain efficiency and rising capacity utilization, which reduces input cost pressures and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Signal yields negative returns in >55% of triggered instances during recovery phases, or predictive accuracy drops below baseline during expansion phases.
